message:
feat(widgets): enhance help registry system

Widget library updates:

**help_registry.rs:**
- Improved help text management
- Better registration/lookup mechanisms
- Support for contextual help

**lib.rs:**
- Export new widget utilities

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Snapshot tests at multiple terminal sizes.

- Cache result and invalidate on layout changes.

- PTY E2E: resize + content changes with JSONL logs.

-{"id":"bd-1rz0.6","title":"Terminal Capability Strategy for Tear‑Free Output","description":"Codify sync‑output vs fallback behavior and ensure safe redraws across muxes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Terminal Capability Strategy for Tear‑Free Output
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Correctness: add property tests for diff/present invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, no-ghosting on shrink)
- Golden outputs: checksum render outputs for canonical fixtures; ensure isomorphism under optimization

@@ -472,7 +472,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-30g1.1","title":"Stateful Trait Definition","description":"Opt-in trait for widgets with persistable state.

## Requirements
- Stateful<S> trait with save/restore
- State key generation (unique per widget)
- Serialization via serde
- Version field for migrations

## Proposed API

```rust
use serde::{Serialize, Deserialize};

pub trait Stateful: Sized {
    /// The state type that gets persisted
    type State: Serialize + for<'de> Deserialize<'de> + Default;
    
    /// Unique key identifying this widget instance
    fn state_key(&self) -> StateKey;
    
    /// Extract current state for persistence
    fn save_state(&self) -> Self::State;
    
    /// Restore state from persistence
    fn restore_state(&mut self, state: Self::State);
    
    /// State schema version for migrations
    fn state_version() -> u32 { 1 }
}

#[derive(Clone, Debug, Hash, Eq, PartialEq)]
pub struct StateKey {
    pub widget_type: &'static str,
    pub instance_id: String,
}

impl StateKey {
    pub fn new(widget_type: &'static str, id: impl Into<String>) -> Self;
    
    /// Generate from widget path in tree
    pub fn from_path(path: &[&str]) -> Self;
}

// Wrapper for versioned state
#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]
struct VersionedState<S> {
    version: u32,
    data: S,
}
```

## Example Implementation

```rust
impl Stateful for ScrollView {
    type State = ScrollViewState;
    
    fn state_key(&self) -> StateKey {
        StateKey::new("ScrollView", &self.id)
    }
    
    fn save_state(&self) -> ScrollViewState {
        ScrollViewState {
            scroll_offset: self.offset,
            content_height: self.content_height,
        }
    }
    
    fn restore_state(&mut self, state: ScrollViewState) {
        self.offset = state.scroll_offset.min(self.max_offset());
    }
}

#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
struct ScrollViewState {
    scroll_offset: u16,
    content_height: u16,
}
```

## File: crates/ftui-widgets/src/state/stateful.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_state_key_uniqueness: Different widgets have different keys
- test_save_restore_cycle: Round-trip preserves state
- test_default_state: Missing state uses Default
- test_version_mismatch: Handled gracefully
- test_partial_state: Missing fields use defaults

## Widgets to Implement
| Widget | State Fields |
|--------|--------------|
| ScrollView | scroll_offset |
| TreeView | expanded_nodes: HashSet<NodeId> |
| Table | sort_column, sort_dir, filter |
| Tabs | active_index |
| SplitView | split_position |

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Stateful Trait Definition
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile; include isomorphism proof for perf changes
- Unit tests: edge cases + regression fixtures; property tests for invariants where applicable
- E2E: deterministic mode + verbose JSONL logs (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums)

 {"id":"bd-30g1.6","title":"State Persistence E2E Test Suite","description":"## Purpose
End-to-end validation of widget state persistence and restoration.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Save/Restore Cycle (persist_cycle_e2e.rs)
- Save on app exit
- Restore on app start
- Partial state handling
- Corrupted state recovery

### 2. Widget State (persist_widget_e2e.rs)
- ScrollView scroll position
- TreeView expanded nodes
- Table sort/filter state
- Tabs active index

### 3. Migration (persist_migrate_e2e.rs)
- Version upgrade handling
- Field addition migration
- Field removal migration
- Type change migration

### 4. Storage Backend (persist_storage_e2e.rs)
- File storage write/read
- Memory storage isolation
- Custom backend integration
- Concurrent access handling

### 5. Error Handling (persist_error_e2e.rs)
- File permission errors
- Disk full handling
- Partial write recovery
- Atomic save verification

## Logging Requirements
- INFO: Save/restore operations with state size
- DEBUG: Migration steps executed
- TRACE: Field-level changes
- Metric: State file size over time

## Script Location
scripts/e2e/state_persistence/

## Crash Recovery Tests
- Kill during save
- Truncated file handling
- Checksum validation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: State Persistence E2E Test Suite
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-30h","title":"Implement Sparkline and Chart widgets","description":"PHASE 7 - EXTRAS: DATA VISUALIZATION

## Background
Sparkline and Chart widgets provide data visualization for metrics, trends, and timeseries. Common requirement for monitoring dashboards and CLI tools.

## Technical Requirements

### Sparkline Widget
- Compact single-line visualization
- Data as Vec<f64> or iterator
- Auto-scaling to min/max range
- Configurable bar character set (▁▂▃▄▅▆▇█)
- Color gradient based on value
- Reference line support (mean, threshold)

### Chart Widget (BarChart, LineChart)
- BarChart:
  - Horizontal/vertical orientation
  - Grouped and stacked modes
  - Labels and value display
  - Color per series
- LineChart:
  - Multiple series support
  - Point markers optional
  - Area fill under line
  - Axis labels and ticks
  - Legend positioning

## Design Rationale
Feature-gated for projects that need visualization. Sparkline is minimal overhead. Full charts use Canvas internally for rendering.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sparkline with auto-scaling
- [ ] Sparkline color gradient
- [ ] BarChart with orientations
- [ ] BarChart grouped/stacked
- [ ] LineChart multi-series
- [ ] LineChart axis rendering
- [ ] Feature gate: charts
